question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R1 removes GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R1↔R2 link, 192.0.0.4/30) from
its OSPF Area 1. R2 still expects an adjacency on the link, so the
neighbor never comes up on R2's side. eBGP between R1 (AS 65001) and
R2 (AS 65000) keeps working — it uses directly-connected next-hops,
not OSPF — but loopback-based reachability into 192.0.2.1 from beyond
R2 is lost. R2 emits OSPF retry / neighbor-init noise on the link.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R1, GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R1<->R2 link) was removed from OSPF area 1.
With one end no longer running OSPF on that interface while R2 still does, the two ends cannot exchange OSPF hellos on the link, so the R1<->R2 adjacency cannot form and the link's prefixes are not exchanged via OSPF. eBGP R1<->R2 keeps working because it uses directly-connected next-hops, but R1's loopback 192.0.2.1 is no longer advertised into OSPF, so its reachability is lost beyond R2.
Fix: re-add GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 to OSPF area 1 on R1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R1: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (area 1) FULL → DOWN reason "interface down or detached".
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.1 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/2 (area 1) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- Bilateral: both ends (R1, R2) report the transition.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 1
no interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 1
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 message-digest-key 1 md5 cisco123

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:41:16.772 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 in area 1 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: interface down or detached, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:41:47.924 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.1 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/2 in area 1 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
